extra.reasoning:
File: /Users/sanjaybasu/clinical-formal-verification/experiments/runs/_judge_prompts/triage-d1-viol-06.txt

The monotonicity property (setting confusion=true never lowers acuity) is VIOLATED.

Counterexample pair (vary only confusion, hold hemoptysis=true, slurred_speech=false):

BEFORE (confusion=false): Only raise_hemoptysis (priority 2) matches -> acuity := urgent. interaction_drop does not fire. Result = urgent (rank 2).

AFTER (confusion=true): Both raise_hemoptysis (priority 2 -> urgent) and interaction_drop (priority 100 -> self_care) match the acuity output. Conflict resolution selects the highest-priority matching rule; interaction_drop at priority 100 wins. Result = self_care (rank 0).

Flipping confusion from false to true drops acuity from urgent (rank 2) down to self_care (rank 0), which is lower, not no-lower. The interaction_drop rule's priority 100 overrides the escalation rules (priority 2), so a confused patient with hemoptysis (or slurred speech) is forced all the way down to self_care. The witness assignment is the AFTER state demonstrating the lowered acuity relative to its confusion=false counterpart. (The same violation also occurs with slurred_speech=true,hemoptysis=false, or both true.)
